description: Extract citation characterizations from 3 published arXiv papers and verify them against the cited works' actual abstracts/claims

papers.title: Towards Verifiable Text Generation with Symbolic References

characterizations.sentence: Classical approaches to this problem consist of designing a template based on the schema of the data, and populating it with the values of a datapoint at runtime (Kukich, 1983; McKeown, 1992; McRoy et al., 2000)
characterizations.claim: Classical data-to-text uses template-based approaches populated at runtime

characterizations.sentence: Neural approaches to data-to-text generation (Wiseman et al., 2017, 2018; Wang, 2019; Yin & Wan, 2022) improve on this by generating text that is datapoint-dependent
characterizations.claim: Neural approaches generate datapoint-dependent text, improving over templates

characterizations.sentence: There is evidence that LLMs are capable of memorizing their training data (Carlini et al., 2019, 2023)
this leads to their struggling to generate counterfactual data that goes against their learnt priors (Hernandez et al., 2023)
characterizations.claim: LLMs struggle to generate counterfactual data

papers.title: Hallucination is Inevitable: An Innate Limitation of Large Language Models

characterizations.sentence: in the survey paper [32], the authors attribute hallucination in natural language generation to heuristic data collection, innate divergence, imperfect representation learning, erroneous decoding, exposure bias, and parametric knowledge bias
characterizations.claim: Ji et al. survey attributes hallucination to 6 factors including data collection, divergence, representation, decoding, exposure bias, parametric bias

characterizations.sentence: factual-centred metrics [21, 22, 47, 63] and benchmarks [39, 40, 72] have been proposed to measure and reduce hallucination on specific datasets
characterizations.claim: Various metrics and benchmarks exist to measure hallucination

characterizations.sentence: Retrieval-based methods reinforce LLM by knowledge graphs or databases to help correct factual errors [63, 81]
characterizations.claim: Retrieval-based methods use KGs/databases to correct factual errors

characterizations.sentence: Prompting the models to reason [76] and verify [15] their answers has also been shown to reduce hallucination
characterizations.claim: Reasoning and self-verification prompting reduces hallucination

characterizations.sentence: A parallel work by Kalai and Vempala [33] provided a statistical lower bound on the rate of hallucination for calibrated LLMs
characterizations.claim: Kalai provides statistical lower bound on hallucination rate for calibrated LLMs
characterizations.AUDIT_NOTE: This is verifiable against Kalai 2024 abstract. Our paper also cites Kalai - cross-check opportunity

characterizations.sentence: It shows a significant fact that pretraining LLMs for predictive accuracy leads to hallucination even with perfect training data
characterizations.claim: Pretraining for predictive accuracy leads to hallucination even with perfect data
characterizations.AUDIT_NOTE: Subtle - Kalai's actual claim is about calibration conditions, not predictive accuracy per se. Possible distortion.

papers.title: FActScore: Fine-grained Atomic Evaluation of Factual Precision in Long Form Text Generation

characterizations.sentence: Lee et al. (2022) evaluates the factual precision with proxy metrics, e.g., whether named entities in a generation appear in an article of the topic
characterizations.claim: Lee et al. use proxy metrics like named entity presence for factual precision

characterizations.sentence: A concurrent work by Manakul et al. (2023) automates the identification of factual errors in LM generations without using any knowledge source
characterizations.claim: SelfCheckGPT identifies factual errors without external knowledge
characterizations.AUDIT_NOTE: Verifiable - SelfCheckGPT uses sampling consistency, not knowledge sources. This characterization is accurate.

characterizations.sentence: Our work is closely related to prior work on fact verification (Thorne et al., 2018; Wadden et al., 2020) where claim sentences are automatically checked against a large knowledge source like Wikipedia
characterizations.claim: FEVER and SciFact check claims against Wikipedia/scientific literature

characterizations.sentence: There also has been work that verifies a longer sentence or text through decomposition to atomic facts (Fan et al., 2020; Wright et al., 2022; Chen et al., 2022; Kamoi et al., 2023)
characterizations.claim: Prior work decomposes longer text into atomic facts for verification

characterizations.sentence: Prior work has used learned models to define automated evaluation scores (Zhang et al., 2020; Liu et al., 2023b)
characterizations.claim: BERTScore and others use learned models for automated evaluation

audit_candidates.characterization: pretraining LLMs for predictive accuracy leads to hallucination even with perfect training data
audit_candidates.concern: Kalai's actual result is about calibration conditions and statistical properties, not 'predictive accuracy' specifically. The Xu paper may be subtly distorting the Kalai result by reframing calibration as predictive accuracy.

audit_candidates.characterization: automates the identification of factual errors in LM generations without using any knowledge source
audit_candidates.concern: SelfCheckGPT uses sampling consistency — it samples multiple outputs and checks agreement. 'Without any knowledge source' is accurate but could mislead about the mechanism.

audit_candidates.characterization: the authors attribute hallucination to heuristic data collection, innate divergence, imperfect representation learning, erroneous decoding, exposure bias, and parametric knowledge bias
audit_candidates.concern: This lists 6 specific factors. Need to verify these are the actual categories from the Ji survey.
